approval.required: before broad deletes, mass renames, irreversible git operations, external publication, secret access, or many long-running subagents
approval.notes: Normal local edits, tests, generated protocol bindings, and workflow artifact updates are allowed.

workflow_policy.reason: Historical per-phase packets made workflow ids look like product step counts and consumed too much context.
workflow_policy.artifact_strategy: write one result file per slice instead of separate contract, implementation, review, verification, and next-slice files
workflow_policy.review_strategy: run local self-review plus $doomsday-review; repeat only for accepted material findings within the bounded review controller
workflow_policy.verification_strategy: run targeted checks during implementation; run full required verification once before commit and repeat only when fixes touch risky shared code
workflow_policy.output_strategy: cap command output by saving long logs outside the workflow and reporting pass/fail plus failure tails

fast_finish_policy.reason: User requested faster execution and lower token burn after the core architecture slices were completed.
rules: Prefer the smallest shippable packet that closes the active concrete gap.

Do not run broad review loops by default; run targeted self-review and only invoke doomsday review when the packet changes architecture boundaries, persistence, lifecycle, security, or repeated checks fail.
Use focused tests, typecheck/build, diff guard, and source-size guard; skip redundant checks that do not cover touched code.
Commit immediately after green verification and workflow result update.
After each commit, select the next concrete gap from current code and plan instead of re-auditing the whole repository.
When docs list an accepted slice as implemented and the code confirms it, sync workflow bookkeeping instead of reimplementing or re-reviewing that slice.
A packet may be audit-only only when it moves the cursor to a concrete implementation gap or proves there is no remaining plan work.

completion_policy.reason: Completion audit found no concrete remaining architecture-plan gap.
rules: Stop the finish-refactor workflow instead of selecting another packet from historical notes.
Treat historical Proposed next slice, Accepted next slice, and ready for review wording as implementation history, not active backlog.
Start any future work from a fresh concrete feature, bug, shell, protocol extension, or audit finding.

top_level_architecture_backlog.summary: Stabilized and committed the ACP session termination split with targeted termination regression tests.
Added a narrow live App Server Protocol stdio runtime path through protocol_edge::RpcGateway.
Implemented fallible storage-backed Task Navigation snapshots and generic committed state_sync publication coverage.

A3 task/list, task/open, task/create, task/send, task/cancel, task/setConfigOption, task/discard, Agent execution, responsive preparation/readiness, Agent config metadata, and slash-command readiness are complete.

A4 Backend-initiated request integration is complete: protocol-edge routing, Agent permission runtime, typed permission/secret/shell method bindings, Frontend/App Shell request bridge, typed waitable producers, task-preparation secret publication, active ACP secret resolver injection, connection-scoped task-update request delivery, backend-owned opaque reveal handles, and VS Code shell-private reveal resolve/open wiring are implemented.

A5a generated permission request typing and typed BackendConnection contract are complete; A5b/A5d VS Code appServer transport respond/request bridge is implemented with envelope unwrapping, structured protocol errors, close cleanup, and timeout cleanup; A5c renderable permission server-request identity is implemented; A5e transitional Frontend protocol-to-render-state mapping is implemented; A5f BackendConnection initialize gate is implemented; A5g App Controller initialize lifecycle and initial snapshot ingestion are implemented; A5h typed Frontend task/list and task/open read path is implemented with legacy fallbacks; A5i safe Frontend task/send and task/cancel mutation paths are implemented with no unsafe legacy replay. A5 is complete.

Move App Shell-owned product decisions into App Server. App Server-owned Agent collection snapshots, catalog startup storage, explicit Custom Agent create/update/replace mutation protocol, live registry refresh, Frontend typed Settings mutation routing, typed Agent Settings details reads, Project identity canonicalization, and legacy Settings fallback Agent status ownership are implemented. Runtime restart/protocol-mode/process-reuse policy is assigned to A7 shared attach-or-launch.

Shared App Server attach-or-launch and reusable LocalHttp transport are implemented through runtime handoff, endpoint discovery/reuse, launch locking, stale cleanup, LocalHttp BackendConnection, and VS Code webview bootstrap.
App Server-owned product modules are in place for Projects, Settings, Agent identity, chat history policy, and recovery policy.

Attachment runtime and file browser lifecycle are complete: pre-send handles, embedded candidates, validation refresh, release, task composer file browser UI, TTL cleanup, send-time consumption, and live same-client open/reveal routing are implemented.

Added typed task/adoptNativeSession protocol support, wired App Server session-load adoption into Task product API, regenerated TypeScript bindings, and changed shared Frontend native-session open to typed requests with local unavailable errors instead of legacy task.create.

Added typed agent/listSessions protocol support, routed it through App Server project-owned workspace resolution, kept raw workspace paths out of typed responses, regenerated TypeScript bindings, and changed shared Frontend navigation session listing to use BackendConnection when connected.

Added typed agent/configOptions and agent/setConfigOption protocol support for pre-task Agent option preparation, routed App Server requests through project-owned workspace resolution, regenerated TypeScript bindings, and changed shared Frontend new-task option loading and updates to use BackendConnection when connected.

Changed connected Settings surface startup to request typed settings/getAgentDetails after initialize and skip legacy settings.snapshot, with helper and mounted-controller coverage; no-App-Server settings startup still uses the dev-host snapshot path.

Changed manual Settings refresh to report an App Server-required error when typed BackendConnection requests are unavailable instead of posting legacy settings.snapshot, matching other Settings mutations.

Added allowLegacyProductMessages to the shared host-message router, disabled it for App Server-connected controllers, ignored legacy product result/error messages in that mode, and kept shell-local messages such as preferences flowing.

Changed shared navigation archive/restore/archive-list and new-task config option callbacks to report App Server connection errors instead of posting legacy task.archive/task.restore/task.list/session.setConfigOption messages.

Changed shared startup navigation/task/settings reads and task-open fallback to report local App Server connection errors instead of posting legacy task.list/task.markRead/task.snapshot/settings.snapshot bridge requests.
Changed shared native-session listing requests to report an App Server connection error when BackendConnection is unavailable instead of posting legacy agent.listSessions bridge requests.

Added typed agent/authenticate protocol bindings, gateway routing, AgentProductApi auth workflow, Frontend Settings auth intent, and focused Rust/Frontend coverage so shared Settings no longer posts legacy agent.authenticate.

Stopped legacy host handlers from issuing follow-up product requests for native-session pagination, post-auth Settings refresh, and task-refresh snapshots, and removed the now-unused host request helpers.

Removed shared host-router ingestion for legacy task, agent, and settings product results/errors; host routing now keeps only shell-local preferences, workspace roots, surface navigation, context-file responses, and tool-detail responses/errors.
Changed new-task config autoload without BackendConnection to a visible App Server error and removed standalone dev-host emulation of legacy product requests.
Trimmed standalone dev-host data to shell-local agents, workspace roots, trace directory, and tool details, removing dead task/session/settings/config fixtures.

Removed dead VS Code webview product refresh/result pushes, runtime notification bridge wiring, stale webview notification contract types, and matching tests after shared Frontend stopped consuming task refresh and Agent catalog/config result messages.

Removed dead VS Code webview product request routes, stale webview product contract variants, unused RuntimeClient wrappers, and obsolete route tests while keeping shell-local chat paging, tool detail, diagnostics, preferences, workspace roots, and App Server transport.
Removed legacy TypeScript runtime method-map entries and unused request/response types for old product bridge methods from app-shell-contracts while preserving shared Frontend render-model types.

Changed the runtime binary default from legacy dotted stdio to App Server Protocol stdio, introduced explicit OPENAIDE_RUNTIME_PROTOCOL=legacy-stdio for remaining shell-local migration calls, and updated VS Code RuntimeProcess.start() to opt into that legacy mode.
Removed the unused webview runtime.health request/result route and contract variants while preserving the VS Code command-side RuntimeClient health method.

Updated VS Code handoff-failure logging, surface test naming, and refactor-plan wording to clarify that no LocalHttp connection means no App Server product transport rather than legacy product bridge fallback.

Added typed runtime settings get/update methods to the App Server Protocol, generated TypeScript bindings, exposed shared ACP trace state through protocol_edge, and routed VS Code developer settings through appServerRequest.

Removed VS Code RuntimeClient runtime.settings/runtime.updateSettings wrappers and app-shell request contracts, added a shared VS Code runtime settings adapter, and moved settings snapshot collection to settings/getRuntime.

Added typed diagnostics/getRuntime App Server Protocol support, generated bindings, exposed storage-backed runtime diagnostics through protocol_edge, and routed VS Code diagnostics export/snapshot through appServerRequest.
Removed unused VS Code RuntimeClient agentProbe, its runtime method constant and test, and stale app-shell legacy agent.probe request/result types.

Added typed task/chatPage protocol method and generated bindings, reused backend ChatItem projection for historical pages, routed VS Code chat.page host messages through appServerRequest, and split TaskProductApi chat paging into its own module to keep production files under 400 LOC.

Added typed task/toolDetail App Server Protocol support for tool artifact details, routed VS Code tool.detail host messages through appServerRequest with a shape mapper for the shared frontend contract, and removed the legacy runtime tool.detail wrapper from VS Code/app-shell runtime contracts.
